mappings.control_id: Improper Check or Handling of Exceptional Conditions
mappings.notes: Agent failures not caught and handled — exception propagates to downstream agents
Cascade fails because upstream agents do not handle failure conditions gracefully
Regex-based input processing causing runaway computation propagating through pipeline
Recursive agent calls without depth limits enabling stack exhaustion cascade
Inject failures to verify circuit breaker activates at threshold; test recovery path
Verify failure in one agent cluster does not propagate to adjacent clusters
Verify monitoring raises alert on cascade indicators before physical impact

mappings.control_id: Cascading agent failure with over-privileged credentials exposes all accessible systems
mappings.notes: Least privilege per agent — cascade blast radius limited by credential scope

mappings.control_id: Shared credentials mean cascade in one agent affects all agents sharing the credential
mappings.notes: Unique identity per agent — cascade cannot leverage shared credentials for lateral movement

mappings.control_id: Long-lived credentials exposed during cascade remain valid for attacker use post-incident
mappings.notes: Short-lived credentials — cascade event triggers automatic revocation of all affected tokens

mappings.control_id: Design circuit breakers, step limits, cost budgets, and human approval gates as explicit security requirements for all agentic automation workflows
